body.title: [sprint] Google Secretary: end-to-end email acceptance test
body.description:
The observable proof-of-life for GoogleSecretaryDevice: a bus command sends a real email from Igor's account to Akien and it arrives. The shim holds credentials for the specific agent identity (Igor's Gmail account) — credentials are not global OAuth, they're per-shim-instance.

Test shape: (1) unit test with MockGoogleClient verifying the command-to-send-email path; (2) integration test (skipped by default, enabled via GOOGLE_SECRETARY_INTEGRATION_TEST=1) that sends a real email from Igor's account to akienm@gmail.com and asserts delivery via IMAP check or manual verification.

**Affected files:** tests/google_secretary/test_acceptance.py (new); devices/google_secretary/shim.py (credential store per-shim); devices/google_secretary/tools/gmail.py (send_email tool)

**Design rules:** no live keys in source; credentials in shim instance config (flat-file or env, gitignored); log every interface crossing (AR-009)

**Scope boundary:** IN — unit test with mock; integration test path with skip guard; shim credential store design. OUT — multi-account credential management (follow-on); scheduling; OAuth setup (human step)

**Completion criteria:** (1) unit test passes with MockGoogleClient; (2) integration test exists with clear skip guard and setup instructions; (3) shim.py has a credential_path field (not hardcoded); (4) no credentials in git diff
